query:
INSERT INTO workspace_notes (workspace_id, author, content, created_at) VALUES
(1, 'Turbo Response', '07/15/2026 — FORENSIC DISCOVERY: IP 89.116.218.6 and the "United States" location tag appear ONLY on September 9, 2025. Every other session in Exhibit B shows a specific city/state. This directly contradicts Robinhood''s claim that the activity is "consistent" with the Claimant. This is the single strongest piece of evidence in the case.', '2026-07-15 00:00:00'),
(1, 'Turbo Response', '07/15/2026 — CORE LEGAL THEORY: Robinhood''s defense relies entirely on the device token hash matching the Claimant''s device. Our counter-theory: (1) A device token proves the device was used, not that the Claimant used it. (2) Network-level anomalies (brand-new IPs, generic location tag) are inconsistent with any prior Claimant session. (3) The 10-second gap between a cancelled sell order and a successful sell order from a new IP at 6:49 AM is a hallmark of automated unauthorized access, not human behavior.', '2026-07-15 00:00:00'),
(1, 'Turbo Response', '07/15/2026 — SETTLEMENT ANALYSIS: Robinhood''s $800 offer is a nuisance settlement designed to make the case go away cheaply. Their legal argument relies entirely on the device token hash, ignoring the network anomalies documented in their own Exhibit B. The $800 offer vs. $1,289 actual loss is a 38-cent-on-the-dollar offer. Claimant countered at $5,500 (loss + damages). Robinhood rejected. Case must proceed to Motion to Compel.', '2026-07-15 00:00:00'),
(1, 'Turbo Response', '07/15/2026 — MOTION TO COMPEL STRATEGY: Focus the motion on the Liquidation Phase at 6:49 AM. Key arguments: (1) FINRA Rule 12507 governs document requests, not interrogatories — Robinhood''s objection is procedurally incorrect. (2) The requested records (wallet approval, device approval logs) are directly relevant to whether the hacker bypassed security. (3) Robinhood cannot claim "consistency" while refusing to produce the very records that would prove or disprove it.', '2026-07-15 00:00:00'),
(1, 'Turbo Response', 'CONTACT NOTE — Simeon Schopf (Simeon.Schopf@robinhood.com): Assistant General Counsel, Litigation and Arbitration at Robinhood. Sent objection email 07/07/2026 citing FINRA Rule 12507(a)(1). Sent settlement rejection 07/15/2026 with $800 counter. Primary point of contact for all Robinhood communications. All correspondence should be documented and preserved as exhibits.', '2026-07-15 00:00:00'),
(1, 'Turbo Response', 'EVIDENCE ANALYSIS — IP PATTERN BREAKDOWN: Pre-September 9 sessions consistently show New York, NY IPs in the 82.x, 83.x, 130.x ranges. September 9 Liquidation Phase shows IP 89.116.218.6 with generic "United States" tag — never seen before or after. September 9 Theft Phase shows IP 23.249.172.42 (New York, NY) — also not in prior session history. The combination of two new IPs in a single day, one with a generic location, is forensically significant.', '2026-07-15 00:00:00'),
(1, 'Turbo Response', 'MISSING EVIDENCE — CRITICAL GAPS: (1) Wallet approval records for the destination USDC address used on Sept 9 — would show whether Claimant ever approved that wallet. (2) Internal Robinhood fraud investigation notes or flags triggered during the 6:49 AM liquidation — Robinhood may have flagged this internally but not disclosed it. (3) Full MFA/2FA logs for Sept 9 — did the session require re-authentication? (4) Device approval records — when was the device token created and from what IP?', '2026-07-15 00:00:00'),
(1, 'Turbo Response', 'RULE REFERENCE — FINRA Rule 12507: Governs document production in arbitration. Robinhood incorrectly characterized discovery requests as "interrogatories" under 12507(a)(1). Document requests are governed by 12507(a)(3). This procedural mischaracterization is itself an argument for the Motion to Compel — Robinhood is using the wrong rule to avoid producing documents.', '2026-07-15 00:00:00');
command:
